title: dalbergia arbutifolia subsp. aberrans
intro: Dalbergia arbutifolia subsp. aberrans, commonly known as the Blackbeech or Brazilian Blackwood, is a subspecies of tree in the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical regions of Brazil. The medicinal properties of Dalbergia arbutifolia subsp. aberrans are attributed to its bark, which contains tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help in reducing stress, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against certain infections. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia arbutifolia subsp. aberrans can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and are often used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
